title: Kernel bootstrap lane: prompt contract. Inspect the local /kernel prompt contract and summarize what this session must maintain while Kernel mode is active. Return 3-5 concise bullets.
summary_text: - Maintain `6+` materially distinct active lanes for the full duration of non-trivial work, with separate execution/exploration, verification/review, and monitoring/risk/context roles; do not silently degrade to single-lane operation. - Fail closed if the environment cannot sustain the Kernel minimum. Parallel sidecars are required even when the main path is serial. - Treat the live lane manifest as a runtime contract: acknowledgements must reflect currently active lanes, not planned lanes, and Kernel is an orchestration mode, not single-agent continuation. - Avoid convenience approvals during bootstrap/local analysis. Use local workspace evidence first, and only seek network/GitHub/escalated actions when explicitly requested or strictly required. - Before any approval or escalated command that could prompt in the TTY, quiesce active lanes; if quiescence cannot be achieved promptly, return `quiescence_timeout` and stop rather than surfacing the prompt.
